metadata.title: States PCS: Uttarakhand GK for UKPSC Exams

questions.question: What is the maximum number of ministers in the Uttarakhand cabinet?
questions.explanation: The 91st Constitutional Amendment Act of 2003 limits state cabinets to 15% of the legislative assembly strength. Uttarakhand has 70 assembly seats, so the maximum number of ministers including the Chief Minister is 12.

questions.question: How many nominated members are in Uttarakhand Legislative Assembly?
questions.explanation: Uttarakhand Legislative Assembly had 1 nominated Anglo-Indian member among 70. The 104th Constitutional Amendment Act, 2020 abolished the Anglo-Indian nominated seat in all Indian Legislative Assemblies, including Uttarakhand.

questions.question: Who started appointing village heads in Uttarakhand’s villages?
questions.explanation: The Chand dynasty ruled parts of present-day Uttarakhand. They established organized village administration with appointed village heads (Gram Pradhan/Mukhia). These leaders managed revenue, justice, and governance, acting as intermediaries for the state. Earlier dynasties like the Katyuris left fewer formal records of such systems. The Chand rulers are historically credited with introducing structured village leadership in Uttarakhand.

questions.question: When did Timur attack Haridwar?
questions.explanation: Timur invaded India in 1398 and attacked Haridwar on 13 January 1399 during his return from Delhi.

questions.question: Which ruler was honored with the title C.S.I. by the British?
questions.explanation: The title Companion of the Order of the Star of India (C.S.I.) was created in 1861 by Queen Victoria to reward Indian rulers loyal to British rule. Narendra Shah was a regional ruler who received this honor for his cooperation with the British administration during their colonial governance.

questions.question: Who founded the Prem Sabha in Kashipur?
questions.explanation: Prem Sabha in Kashipur was founded by Pt. Govind Ballabh Pant around 1914 as a literary and social organisation. It focused on social reform, education, and public awareness in the Kumaon region. Pant was an early freedom fighter and later became the first Chief Minister of Uttar Pradesh and India’s Home Minister.

questions.question: With which empire is King Somachandra associated?
questions.explanation: Historical records and genealogies of Pala, Chandra, and Yadava empires do not mention King Somachandra. Verified sources do not associate him with any of these empires.

questions.question: What causes most future earthquakes in Uttarakhand’s Himalayan region?

options.label: B
options.text: Both tectonic plate movement and faults

answer.label: B
answer.text: Both tectonic plate movement and faults

questions.explanation: Most earthquakes in the Himalayas, including Uttarakhand, result from the collision of the Indian and Eurasian tectonic plates. This collision causes compression and crustal shortening, which occur along major faults like the Main Himalayan Thrust. Both tectonic plate movement and fault activity contribute to seismicity and earthquake occurrence in the region.

questions.question: What is the total area of Uttarakhand?
questions.explanation: Uttarakhand covers an area of 53,483 square kilometers. It was formed on November 9, 2000, carved out from Uttar Pradesh. The state consists of two administrative divisions: Garhwal and Kumaon. Approximately 86% of Uttarakhand’s area is mountainous, and about 65% is forested.

questions.question: Which Himalayan range remains snow-covered throughout the year?
questions.explanation: The Great Himalaya is the highest Himalayan range with peaks above 6,000–8,000 meters. Temperatures remain below freezing there most of the year, causing permanent snow and glaciers. This range feeds many major perennial rivers and stays snowbound throughout the year due to its height and latitude near the Tropic of Cancer.
